reasoning: The Bahamas essentially experiences 0 events of Explosions/Remote violence each year, according to ACLED data (having a current 360-day average of 0). The threshold requires more than 10 times the baseline metric, which equates to 11 or more such events in a 30-day period. This is an extraordinarily high threshold for a country that has virtually zero baseline incidents of remote violence, artillery, airstrikes, or mass IED sequences. Even factoring in standard gang violence, reaching 11+ remote explosive acts within a single month is extremely improbable and would imply a massive unexpected descent into civil war or sustained terrorist bombing campaigns, for which there are no indicators. Thus, the probabilities sit at the minimum bound allowed (starting at 0.05) and scaling up marginally over time to account for baseline uncertainty horizons.
